description_long: This sculpture depicts a life-sized human hand, rendered in a dynamic, partially open gesture as if reaching or grasping. The surface is textured and mottled with a mix of earthy browns, reds, and golds, giving it a weathered, metallic appearance. The hand is mounted upright on a rectangular plinth, which appears to be made of the same material and coloration as the hand itself. The fingers are splayed, with the thumb and index finger forming a loose circle, while the other fingers are slightly curled. The base features an inscription, suggesting the presence of a title or artist's name. The overall form is expressive and detailed, capturing the anatomical structure and movement of a real human hand.
description: A life-sized, textured sculpture of a human hand in a dynamic, partially open gesture, mounted on a rectangular plinth with an earthy, metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the palm and splayed fingers, with the inscription on the base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thumb and the curvature of the hand, with the fingers forming a loose circle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hand and the full extension of the fingers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the pinky and ring fingers, with the thumb partially obscured.
attribute_location_context: No specific environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a white background.
